title: Food allergy endotypes revisited.
author_string: Baker, Mary Grace, Wong, Lydia Su Yin, Konstantinou, George N, Nowak-Wegrzyn, Anna
journal_full: The Journal of allergy and clinical immunology
abstract: In the last century, food allergy has become recognized as an increasingly prevalent and heterogeneous condition. Advances in biomedical technology have revealed complex genetic, environmental, immune, and metabolic pathways underlying the pathogenesis of food-allergic disorders. These findings permit classification of distinct food allergy endotypes with unique pathophysiologic features. In this review, we suggest that these endotypes of food-allergic disorders should be defined on the basis of (1) whether or not the allergic antibody IgE plays an essential role in disease pathogenesis, (2) the molecular features of the allergen (protein vs carbohydrate), and (3) the molecular markers associated with prognosis, severity, or clinical presentation. Beyond these broad categories, additional subtypes with unique mechanistic characteristics are discussed.
